questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: An airway of cross-section $12\,\text{m}^2$ carries air at $2.5\,\text{m/s}$. The air quantity is _____ m$^3$/s.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: An airway of area $10\,\text{m}^2$ carries $40\,\text{m}^3$/s of air. The mean velocity is _____ m/s.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: To pass $60\,\text{m}^3$/s at a limiting velocity of $5\,\text{m/s}$, the airway cross-section must be at least _____ m$^2$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The square law of mine ventilation relates pressure $P$, resistance $R$ and quantity $Q$ as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For turbulent mine airflow the pressure loss varies as the square of the quantity.

**Working.** $P=RQ^2$.

**Answer.** $P=R\,Q^2$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: An airway has resistance $0.5\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ and carries $10\,\text{m}^3$/s. The pressure drop is _____ Pa.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A pressure drop of $200\,\text{Pa}$ accompanies a flow of $20\,\text{m}^3$/s. The airway resistance is _____ Ns$^2$/m$^8$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: An airway of resistance $0.2\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ has a pressure drop of $80\,\text{Pa}$. The quantity flowing is _____ m$^3$/s.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: In SI units, the Atkinson resistance of a mine airway is expressed in
questions.solution:
**Concept.** From $P=RQ^2$, $R$ has units of $\text{Pa}\,/(\text{m}^3/\text{s})^2=\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$.

**Working.** Dimensional analysis gives Ns$^2$/m$^8$.

**Answer.** Ns$^2$/m$^8$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Using $R=\dfrac{k\,P\,L}{A^3}$ with friction factor $k=0.01\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^4$, perimeter $P=10\,\text{m}$, length $L=500\,\text{m}$ and area $A=6\,\text{m}^2$, the airway resistance is _____ Ns$^2$/m$^8$. Round to two decimals.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: An airway of resistance $0.3\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ carries $15\,\text{m}^3$/s. The pressure loss is _____ Pa.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Atkinson's friction factor $k$ for an airway depends mainly on
options: the roughness and degree of lining of the airway surface
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $k$ is a property of the airway surface, not the flow.

**Working.** Rougher, unlined airways have larger $k$.

**Answer.** the roughness and lining of the airway surface.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan develops $500\,\text{Pa}$ while passing $40\,\text{m}^3$/s. The air power is _____ W.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan develops $300\,\text{Pa}$ while passing $50\,\text{m}^3$/s. The air power is _____ kW.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Air power $=P\,Q$ (W), then divide by $1000$ for kW.

**Working.**
$$\frac{300\times50}{1000}=\frac{15000}{1000}=15.$$

**Answer.** $15\,\text{kW}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan delivers $30\,\text{m}^3$/s at $600\,\text{Pa}$ with an overall efficiency of $75\%$. The motor input power is _____ W.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Mine ventilation pressure is conventionally expressed in
options: pascals (Pa) or millimetres of water gauge
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Ventilation pressure is a static head.

**Working.** It is measured in Pa or mm of water gauge.

**Answer.** pascals or mm water gauge.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A water-gauge reading of $50\,\text{mm}$ corresponds to _____ Pa (take $1\,\text{mm w.g.}=9.81\,\text{Pa}$).

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: For air of density $1.2\,\text{kg/m}^3$ moving at $10\,\text{m/s}$, the velocity pressure is _____ Pa.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: At a point the static pressure is $200\,\text{Pa}$ and the velocity pressure is $60\,\text{Pa}$. The total pressure is _____ Pa.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: If an airway's cross-sectional area is reduced to half while the quantity stays constant, the air velocity
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Q=Av$ is constant, so $v\propto1/A$.

**Working.** Halving $A$ doubles $v$.

**Answer.** doubles.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A flow of $30\,\text{m}^3$/s passes from a $10\,\text{m}^2$ section into a $5\,\text{m}^2$ section. The velocity in the smaller section is _____ m/s.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Air of density $1.2\,\text{kg/m}^3$ flows at $50\,\text{m}^3$/s. The mass flow rate is _____ kg/s.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The standard density of mine air used in ventilation calculations is approximately
questions.solution:
**Concept.** At normal temperature and pressure, air density is about $1.2\,\text{kg/m}^3$.

**Working.** This is the conventional standard value.

**Answer.** 1.2 kg/m$^3$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The equivalent orifice is $A=\dfrac{1.19\,Q}{\sqrt{P}}$ (SI). For $Q=20\,\text{m}^3$/s against $P=400\,\text{Pa}$, the equivalent orifice is _____ m$^2$. Round to two decimals.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A small equivalent orifice for a mine indicates that the mine is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A small orifice represents a large restriction.

**Working.** High resistance means the mine is hard to ventilate.

**Answer.** difficult to ventilate.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: An airway loses $50\,\text{Pa}$ at a certain flow. If the quantity is doubled, the new pressure loss is _____ Pa.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: An airway carries $20\,\text{m}^3$/s. To halve the pressure loss, the quantity must be reduced to _____ m$^3$/s. Round to two decimals.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The Atkinson resistance of a given airway is essentially independent of
options: the air quantity flowing through it
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $R=kPL/A^3$ depends only on geometry and friction.

**Working.** It does not depend on the flow rate $Q$.

**Answer.** the air quantity flowing through it.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: An airway has a pressure loss of $60\,\text{Pa}$. If only the friction factor $k$ is doubled (flow unchanged), the new pressure loss is _____ Pa.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A resistance of $0.25\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ is quoted at standard density $1.2\,\text{kg/m}^3$. Corrected to a density of $1.0\,\text{kg/m}^3$ (resistance scales with $\rho$), it becomes _____ Ns$^2$/m$^8$. Round to two decimals.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: In $R=\dfrac{kPL}{A^3}$, if only the cross-sectional area $A$ is doubled (perimeter assumed unchanged), the resistance decreases by a factor of

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: An airway of resistance $1.6\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ has its area doubled (perimeter unchanged). The new resistance is _____ Ns$^2$/m$^8$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A rectangular airway is $4\,\text{m}$ wide and $3\,\text{m}$ high. Its perimeter is _____ m.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A rectangular airway is $4\,\text{m}$ wide and $3\,\text{m}$ high. Its cross-sectional area is _____ m$^2$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: For an airway of area $12\,\text{m}^2$ and perimeter $14\,\text{m}$, the hydraulic mean depth $m=A/P$ is _____ m. Round to two decimals.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The minimum air velocity to be maintained at a working face is governed mainly by
options: statutory limits and the need to dilute gas, dust and heat
the depth of the shaft only
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Face velocity must remove contaminants and heat.

**Working.** Statutory minima and dilution requirements set it.

**Answer.** statutory limits and dilution of gas/dust/heat.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: For a given fan running at variable speed $N$, the quantity delivered varies as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** First fan law.

**Working.** Quantity is directly proportional to speed.

**Answer.** $Q\propto N$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: For a given fan running at variable speed $N$, the pressure developed varies as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Second fan law.

**Working.** Pressure is proportional to the square of speed.

**Answer.** $P\propto N^2$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: For a given fan running at variable speed $N$, the power absorbed varies as
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Third fan law.

**Working.** Power is proportional to the cube of speed.

**Answer.** $W\propto N^3$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan delivers $40\,\text{m}^3$/s at $600\,\text{rpm}$. At $720\,\text{rpm}$ it delivers _____ m$^3$/s.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan develops $500\,\text{Pa}$ at $600\,\text{rpm}$. At $720\,\text{rpm}$ it develops _____ Pa.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan absorbs $20\,\text{kW}$ at $600\,\text{rpm}$. At $720\,\text{rpm}$ it absorbs _____ kW. Round to two decimals.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: If a fan's speed is increased by $10\%$, the quantity delivered increases by _____ percent.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $Q\propto N$.

**Working.** A $10\%$ rise in $N$ gives a $10\%$ rise in $Q$.

**Answer.** $10\%$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: If a fan's speed is increased by $10\%$, the pressure increases by _____ percent.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: If a fan's speed is increased by $10\%$, the power absorbed increases by _____ percent. Round to one decimal.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The operating point of a main fan is found at
options: the intersection of the fan characteristic curve and the mine resistance curve
the peak of the fan curve
the maximum efficiency point regardless of resistance
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Steady operation requires fan pressure to equal mine pressure loss.

**Working.** This occurs where the two curves cross.

**Answer.** the intersection of the fan and mine resistance curves.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan has the linear characteristic $P=400-20Q$ (Pa) and the mine resistance is $R=2\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ (so $P=2Q^2$). The operating quantity is _____ m$^3$/s.

figure.caption: Fan characteristic meets the mine resistance curve at the operating point.

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Set fan pressure equal to mine pressure: $400-20Q=2Q^2$.

**Working.**
$$2Q^2+20Q-400=0\Rightarrow Q^2+10Q-200=0\Rightarrow Q=\frac{-10+\sqrt{100+800}}{2}=\frac{-10+30}{2}=10.$$

**Answer.** $10\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: At the operating point of the previous fan ($Q=10\,\text{m}^3$/s, $R=2$), the operating pressure is _____ Pa.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Compared with an axial-flow fan, a centrifugal (radial) fan generally produces
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Centrifugal fans suit high-resistance circuits.

**Working.** They develop higher pressure but pass less air than axial fans of similar size.

**Answer.** higher pressure at lower quantity.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Most main mine fans are operated as exhaust (induced) fans, which keep the workings
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An exhausting fan draws air out of the upcast.

**Working.** This holds the workings just below atmospheric pressure, aiding gas drainage.

**Answer.** slightly below atmospheric pressure.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan has a static pressure of $480\,\text{Pa}$ and a velocity pressure of $20\,\text{Pa}$. Its total pressure is _____ Pa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Fan total pressure $=$ static $+$ velocity pressure.

**Working.**
$$480+20=500.$$

**Answer.** $500\,\text{Pa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan has a static pressure of $480\,\text{Pa}$, passes $40\,\text{m}^3$/s and draws $24\,\text{kW}$ at the shaft. Its static efficiency is _____ percent.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two identical fans, each passing $30\,\text{m}^3$/s against a fixed pressure, are coupled in parallel against a low-resistance circuit. The combined quantity (ideal) is _____ m$^3$/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Parallel fans add quantities at a common pressure.

**Working.**
$$30+30=60.$$

**Answer.** $60\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two identical fans coupled in series against a high-resistance circuit (ideal) deliver
options: double the pressure at the same quantity
double the quantity at the same pressure
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Series fans add pressures.

**Working.** Each handles the same quantity; pressures sum.

**Answer.** double the pressure at the same quantity.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two identical fans coupled in parallel against a low-resistance circuit (ideal) deliver
options: double the quantity at the same pressure
double the pressure at the same quantity
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Parallel fans add quantities.

**Working.** Each develops the same pressure; quantities sum.

**Answer.** double the quantity at the same pressure.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Downcast air has density $1.2\,\text{kg/m}^3$ and upcast air $1.1\,\text{kg/m}^3$ over a shaft depth of $200\,\text{m}$. The natural ventilation pressure ($\text{NVP}=(\rho_d-\rho_u)gH$, $g=9.81$) is _____ Pa. Round to one decimal.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Natural ventilation in a mine arises from
options: the density difference between the downcast and upcast air columns
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A heavier downcast column displaces the lighter upcast column.

**Working.** The resulting buoyancy drives the airflow.

**Answer.** density difference between downcast and upcast columns.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan develops $600\,\text{Pa}$ and the natural ventilation pressure of $100\,\text{Pa}$ acts in the same direction. The total motive pressure is _____ Pa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Aiding NVP adds to fan pressure.

**Working.**
$$600+100=700.$$

**Answer.** $700\,\text{Pa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Using $\rho=\dfrac{P}{RT}$ with $P=100{,}000\,\text{Pa}$, $R=287\,\text{J/kg\,K}$ and $T=300\,\text{K}$, the air density is _____ kg/m$^3$. Round to two decimals.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: At constant pressure, as the temperature of mine air increases its density
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\rho=\dfrac{P}{RT}$, so $\rho\propto1/T$ at fixed $P$.

**Working.** Higher $T$ lowers $\rho$.

**Answer.** decreases.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: For downcast density $1.18\,\text{kg/m}^3$, upcast $1.10\,\text{kg/m}^3$ and depth $150\,\text{m}$, the natural ventilation pressure ($g=9.81$) is _____ Pa. Round to one decimal.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A booster fan is installed
options: underground within a split to raise airflow in a high-resistance district
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Boosters supplement the main fan locally.

**Working.** They overcome high resistance in a particular split.

**Answer.** underground within a split to raise airflow.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan delivers $50\,\text{m}^3$/s at $500\,\text{rpm}$. To deliver $60\,\text{m}^3$/s, the required speed is _____ rpm.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan develops $400\,\text{Pa}$ at $500\,\text{rpm}$. At $600\,\text{rpm}$ it develops _____ Pa.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan absorbs $25\,\text{kW}$ at $500\,\text{rpm}$. At $600\,\text{rpm}$ it absorbs _____ kW. Round to one decimal.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The characteristic curve of an axial-flow fan typically exhibits, at low quantity, a
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Axial fans can stall at low flow.

**Working.** The curve dips, giving unstable operation in that zone.

**Answer.** a stall (dip) region.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan delivers an air power of $18\,\text{kW}$ while its shaft power is $22.5\,\text{kW}$. The fan efficiency is _____ percent.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan must deliver an air power of $16\,\text{kW}$ at an efficiency of $80\%$. The shaft power required is _____ kW.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: If the resistance of a mine increases (a steeper resistance curve), the fan operating point shifts to
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A steeper resistance curve intersects the fan curve higher up.

**Working.** Quantity falls and pressure rises.

**Answer.** lower quantity and higher pressure.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A regulator adding $0.5\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ of resistance to a split carrying $20\,\text{m}^3$/s imposes an extra pressure drop of _____ Pa.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A regulator (an adjustable door opening) in a ventilation split is used to
options: add resistance and so reduce/control the airflow in that split
increase the airflow without a fan
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A regulator restricts a split.

**Working.** Added resistance lowers and balances the flow.

**Answer.** add resistance to reduce/control the airflow.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: For ventilation airways connected in series, the equivalent resistance is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Series airways carry the same quantity; their pressure drops add.

**Working.** $R_{eq}=\sum R_i$.

**Answer.** $R_{eq}=R_1+R_2+\dots$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two airways of resistance $2$ and $3\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ are in series. The equivalent resistance is _____ Ns$^2$/m$^8$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A series circuit of equivalent resistance $5\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ carries $10\,\text{m}^3$/s. The total pressure drop is _____ Pa.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: For two airways in parallel, the equivalent resistance satisfies
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Parallel airways share the same pressure; $Q=\sqrt{P/R}$, and quantities add.

**Working.** $\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{R_{eq}}}=\sum\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{R_i}}$.

**Answer.** the reciprocal-root relation.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two airways, each of resistance $4\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$, are in parallel. The equivalent resistance is _____ Ns$^2$/m$^8$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two airways of resistance $4$ and $9\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ are in parallel. The equivalent resistance is _____ Ns$^2$/m$^8$. Round to two decimals.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A parallel combination of equivalent resistance $1\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ has a pressure drop of $400\,\text{Pa}$. The total quantity is _____ m$^3$/s.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Total flow of $20\,\text{m}^3$/s divides between two parallel airways of equal resistance. Each branch carries _____ m$^3$/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Equal resistances split the flow equally.

**Working.**
$$\frac{20}{2}=10.$$

**Answer.** $10\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Kirchhoff's first (node) law applied to a ventilation network states that at any junction
options: the algebraic sum of the air quantities is zero
the algebraic sum of pressures is zero
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Mass (volume) is conserved at a node.

**Working.** Inflow equals outflow, so $\sum Q=0$ algebraically.

**Answer.** the algebraic sum of quantities is zero.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Kirchhoff's second (mesh) law applied to a ventilation network states that around any closed mesh
options: the algebraic sum of pressure drops (including fan/NVP) is zero
the algebraic sum of quantities is zero
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Pressure is single-valued around a loop.

**Working.** Drops and sources sum to zero around a mesh.

**Answer.** the algebraic sum of pressure drops is zero.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: At a junction, $30$ and $20\,\text{m}^3$/s enter and one branch carries away $25\,\text{m}^3$/s. The remaining branch carries _____ m$^3$/s.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A total of $30\,\text{m}^3$/s splits between parallel airways of resistance $1$ and $4\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$. The quantity in the lower-resistance ($R=1$) airway is _____ m$^3$/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** In parallel, $Q\propto\dfrac{1}{\sqrt R}$, so $\dfrac{Q_1}{Q_2}=\sqrt{\dfrac{R_2}{R_1}}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{Q_1}{Q_2}=\sqrt{\frac{4}{1}}=2,\quad Q_1+Q_2=30\Rightarrow Q_1=20,\ Q_2=10.$$

**Answer.** $20\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two parallel branches share a common pressure drop of $400\,\text{Pa}$. The branch of resistance $4\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ carries _____ m$^3$/s.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: In a parallel (split) ventilation circuit, the pressure drop across each branch is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Branches share common end nodes.

**Working.** The pressure difference across them is identical.

**Answer.** the same for every branch.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: In a series ventilation circuit, the quantity flowing through each airway is
questions.solution:
**Concept.** There is one continuous path.

**Working.** The same air passes through each airway in turn.

**Answer.** the same in every airway.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Three airways of resistance $1$, $2$ and $3\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ are in series. The equivalent resistance is _____ Ns$^2$/m$^8$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two airways of $4\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ each are in parallel, and this group is in series with a $2\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ airway. The overall equivalent resistance is _____ Ns$^2$/m$^8$.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Reduce the parallel pair, then add in series.

**Working.**
$$R_{\parallel}=1\ (\text{from }0.5+0.5),\quad R_{eq}=1+2=3.$$

**Answer.** $3\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The previous network ($R_{eq}=3\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$) carries $10\,\text{m}^3$/s. The total pressure drop is _____ Pa.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Driving an additional airway in parallel with an existing district
options: removes the need for a fan
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Parallel paths offer more area for flow.

**Working.** Equivalent resistance falls.

**Answer.** reduces the overall resistance.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two airways, each of resistance $2\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$, are in parallel. The equivalent resistance is _____ Ns$^2$/m$^8$.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For two equal resistances in parallel, $R_{eq}=R/4$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{2}{4}=0.5.$$

**Answer.** $0.5\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Three airways, each of resistance $9\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$, are in parallel. The equivalent resistance is _____ Ns$^2$/m$^8$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Splitting the air (parallel districts) rather than coursing it in series is preferred because it
options: lowers resistance and supplies fresh air to each district
concentrates all gas in one airway
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Splitting gives each district intake-quality air.

**Working.** It also reduces total resistance and power.

**Answer.** lowers resistance and supplies fresh air to each district.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A total of $60\,\text{m}^3$/s splits naturally between parallel airways of resistance $1$ and $3\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$. The quantity in the $R=1$ airway is _____ m$^3$/s. Round to two decimals.

figure.caption: Air splits between a low-resistance (R=1) and a high-resistance (R=3) parallel route.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: To force an equal split of $30\,\text{m}^3$/s through each of two parallel airways of resistance $1$ and $3\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$, a regulator is placed in the $R=1$ airway. The regulator pressure drop required is _____ Pa.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Both branches must have the same total pressure drop. Add the deficit to the low-resistance branch.

**Working.**
$$P_{R=3}=3\times30^2=2700,\quad P_{R=1}=1\times30^2=900.$$
$$\Delta P_{\text{reg}}=2700-900=1800.$$

**Answer.** $1800\,\text{Pa}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The purpose of a controlled split is to
options: distribute air in desired proportions to districts using regulators
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Districts need air in proportion to their gas/heat load.

**Working.** Regulators adjust each split to the required quantity.

**Answer.** distribute air in desired proportions using regulators.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two parallel branches share a pressure drop of $200\,\text{Pa}$. The branch of resistance $0.5\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ carries _____ m$^3$/s.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: If the two parallel branches above carry $10$ and $20\,\text{m}^3$/s, the total quantity is _____ m$^3$/s.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The equivalent resistance of a complex ventilation network is most simply obtained by
options: successively reducing series and parallel groups
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Networks reduce stepwise.

**Working.** Combine series and parallel sub-groups until one resistance remains.

**Answer.** successively reducing series and parallel groups.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two airways of resistance $0.4$ and $0.6\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ are in series. The equivalent resistance is _____ Ns$^2$/m$^8$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two airways of $1\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ each are in parallel, and that group is in series with $0.75\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$. At a pressure drop of $400\,\text{Pa}$, the total quantity is _____ m$^3$/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Reduce the network, then apply $Q=\sqrt{P/R_{eq}}$.

**Working.**
$$R_{\parallel}=\frac{1}{4}=0.25,\quad R_{eq}=0.25+0.75=1.0,\quad Q=\sqrt{\frac{400}{1.0}}=20.$$

**Answer.** $20\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two airways of equal resistance $R$ in parallel have an equivalent resistance of
questions.solution:
**Concept.** $\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{R_{eq}}}=\dfrac{2}{\sqrt R}\Rightarrow R_{eq}=R/4$.

**Working.** Two equal parallel airways quarter the resistance.

**Answer.** $R/4$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two airways of resistance $8\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ each are in parallel. The equivalent resistance is _____ Ns$^2$/m$^8$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: When flow divides between two parallel airways of equal resistance, each carries _____ percent of the total.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Equal resistances split flow equally.

**Working.** Each branch takes half.

**Answer.** $50\%$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Serving each ventilation district by its own split improves safety because
options: contaminated air from one district does not pass into another
it eliminates the need for surveys
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Independent splits keep returns separate.

**Working.** A gas or fire problem in one district is contained.

**Answer.** contaminated air from one district does not pass into another.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The pressure required to pass $40\,\text{m}^3$/s through a network of equivalent resistance $0.5\,\text{Ns}^2/\text{m}^8$ is _____ Pa.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: In Indian coal mines, persons must be withdrawn if the methane content in the general body of return air reaches
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Statutory methane limits guard against ignition.

**Working.** Withdrawal is required at $1.25\%$ in the general body of return air.

**Answer.** 1.25%.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at $0.05\,\text{m}^3$/s. To keep its concentration at $1\%$ (intake methane-free), the air quantity required is _____ m$^3$/s.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: For the same methane emission of $0.05\,\text{m}^3$/s, the air needed to hold the concentration to $0.5\%$ is _____ m$^3$/s.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Because methane is lighter than air, it tends to
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Specific gravity of methane is about $0.55$.

**Working.** Being buoyant, it layers along the roof.

**Answer.** accumulate near the roof.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Return air of $4\,\text{m}^3$/s carries $0.02\,\text{m}^3$/s of methane. The methane concentration is _____ percent.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The flammable (explosive) range of methane in air is approximately
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Methane explodes within fixed concentration limits.

**Working.** The range is roughly $5\%$ (lower) to $15\%$ (upper), most violent near $9.5\%$.

**Answer.** 5% to 15%.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Carbon dioxide is emitted at $0.03\,\text{m}^3$/s. To hold its concentration to $0.5\%$, the air required is _____ m$^3$/s.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Carbon monoxide is especially dangerous because it
options: combines with haemoglobin and prevents oxygen transport in blood
is heavier than air and settles
questions.solution:
**Concept.** CO has a high affinity for haemoglobin.

**Working.** It forms carboxyhaemoglobin, starving tissues of oxygen.

**Answer.** it combines with haemoglobin, preventing oxygen transport.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A carbon-monoxide concentration of $50\,\text{ppm}$ expressed as a percentage is _____ percent. (Take $1\%=10{,}000\,\text{ppm}$.)

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A flame safety lamp is traditionally used in a mine to
options: detect methane (by cap height) and oxygen deficiency
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The flame responds to gas in air.

**Working.** A methane cap appears above the flame; a low flame indicates oxygen deficiency.

**Answer.** detect methane and oxygen deficiency.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Methane is emitted at $0.04\,\text{m}^3$/s into intake air that already contains $0.2\%$ methane. To keep the return at $1\%$, the air quantity required is _____ m$^3$/s.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Blackdamp (stythe) in a mine is mainly
options: a mixture of carbon dioxide and nitrogen (oxygen-deficient air)
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Blackdamp is the residual non-breathable air.

**Working.** It is rich in CO$_2$ and N$_2$ and low in O$_2$.

**Answer.** carbon dioxide and nitrogen.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Afterdamp, the gas mixture left following an explosion or fire, chiefly contains
options: carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and nitrogen
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Combustion consumes oxygen and yields toxic products.

**Working.** Afterdamp is dominated by CO, CO$_2$ and N$_2$.

**Answer.** carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide and nitrogen.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The oxygen content of normal fresh air is approximately _____ percent by volume.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Atmospheric composition.

**Working.** Fresh air contains about $20.9\%$ oxygen (and $\approx79\%$ nitrogen).

**Answer.** $\approx 20.9\%$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Air at $60\,\text{kg/s}$ ($c_p=1.005\,\text{kJ/kg\,K}$) is warmed by $5\,\text{K}$ as it passes through a district. The sensible heat removed is _____ kW. Round to one decimal.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The cooling power of mine air (its capacity to cool the human body) is assessed with a
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The kata thermometer measures cooling rate.

**Working.** Its cooling time indicates the cooling power of the air.

**Answer.** kata thermometer.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The wet-bulb temperature of mine air is measured chiefly to assess
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Wet-bulb temperature reflects evaporative cooling.

**Working.** It is the key index of heat stress in humid workings.

**Answer.** heat stress and humidity.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: When the wet-bulb temperature equals the dry-bulb temperature, the relative humidity is _____ percent.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** No evaporative depression means saturated air.

**Working.** Equal wet- and dry-bulb readings imply $100\%$ RH.

**Answer.** $100\%$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: As the humidity of mine air rises, the body's ability to lose heat by sweating
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Evaporation slows as air approaches saturation.

**Working.** High humidity reduces sweat evaporation and cooling.

**Answer.** decreases.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: If $6\,\text{m}^3$/min of air is required per person and $50$ persons work in a district, the minimum air requirement is _____ m$^3$/s.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Total $=$ rate per person $\times$ number, converted to per second.

**Working.**
$$\frac{6\times50}{60}=\frac{300}{60}=5.$$

**Answer.** $5\,\text{m}^3/\text{s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Auxiliary ventilation is needed to ventilate
options: blind headings and development faces not on the main through-circuit
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Dead-end headings have no through flow.

**Working.** Auxiliary fans and ducts carry air to such faces.

**Answer.** blind headings/development faces.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: In a forcing auxiliary ventilation system, fresh air is delivered to the face
options: through a duct with the fan at the fresh-air (intake) end
questions.solution:
**Concept.** A forcing system blows air to the face.

**Working.** The fan draws intake air and forces it down the duct.

**Answer.** through a duct with the fan at the fresh-air end.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A circular duct of diameter $0.6\,\text{m}$ carries air at $10\,\text{m/s}$. The quantity delivered is _____ m$^3$/s. Round to two decimals.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Leakage along an auxiliary ventilation duct
options: reduces the air quantity reaching the face
increases the quantity at the face
has no effect on the face air
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Air escaping through joints never reaches the face.

**Working.** Leakage lowers face quantity and efficiency.

**Answer.** reduces the air quantity reaching the face.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A commonly applied respirable-dust threshold limit value in mines is about _____ mg/m$^3$.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Respirable dust limits protect against pneumoconiosis.

**Working.** A typical TLV for respirable dust is about $3\,\text{mg/m}^3$.

**Answer.** $\approx 3\,\text{mg/m}^3$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Respirable dust, which reaches the alveoli and causes pneumoconiosis, consists mainly of particles
options: visible to the naked eye only
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Only fine particles penetrate deep into the lungs.

**Working.** Respirable dust is generally finer than $5\,\mu\text{m}$.

**Answer.** below about 5 microns.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A vane anemometer is used to measure
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The vane rotates in proportion to air speed.

**Working.** It reads the velocity of the airflow.

**Answer.** air velocity.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: An anemometer reads a mean velocity of $3\,\text{m/s}$ across an airway of $8\,\text{m}^2$. The air quantity is _____ m$^3$/s.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A pitot-static tube in an airway measures
options: velocity pressure, from which air velocity is computed
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The pitot tube senses the difference between total and static pressure.

**Working.** This velocity pressure gives the air velocity.

**Answer.** velocity pressure (hence velocity).

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A pitot tube reads a velocity pressure of $60\,\text{Pa}$ in air of density $1.2\,\text{kg/m}^3$. The air velocity ($v=\sqrt{2p_v/\rho}$) is _____ m/s.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The figure shows two airways connected in series between junctions A and C, of resistances $R_1=2$ and $R_2=3\ \mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$. The equivalent resistance of the path A–C, in $\mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$, is _____.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The figure shows two airways in parallel between junctions A and B, each of resistance $4\ \mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$. Using the square law, the equivalent resistance of the parallel combination, in $\mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$, is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** For parallel airways under the square law $\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{R_{eq}}}=\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{R_1}}+\dfrac{1}{\sqrt{R_2}}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{1}{\sqrt{R_{eq}}}=\frac{1}{\sqrt{4}}+\frac{1}{\sqrt{4}}=\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{2}=1\Rightarrow R_{eq}=1\ \mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$$

**Answer.** $1\ \mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan characteristic $p=300-10Q$ (Pa) operates against a mine of resistance giving $p=2Q^2$ (Pa), as shown. The operating-point quantity Q, in $\mathrm{m^3/s}$, is _____.

figure.caption: Fan characteristic and mine resistance curve

questions.solution:
**Concept.** At the operating point the fan pressure equals the mine resistance pressure: $a-bQ=rQ^2$.

**Working.**
$$2Q^2+10Q-300=0\Rightarrow Q^2+5Q-150=0$$
$$Q=\frac{-5+\sqrt{25+600}}{2}=\frac{-5+25}{2}=10$$

**Answer.** $10\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A water-filled U-tube manometer connected across a door shows a level difference $h=50\ \text{mm}$ (see figure). Taking water density $1000\ \mathrm{kg/m^3}$ and $g=9.81\ \mathrm{m/s^2}$, the pressure difference $p=\rho g h$, in Pa, is _____ (one decimal).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><path d='M50 20 L50 130 L130 130 L130 20' fill='none' stroke='#334155' stroke-width='3'/><rect x='44' y='70' width='12' height='60' fill='#60a5fa'/><rect x='124' y='100' width='12' height='30' fill='#60a5fa'/><line x1='56' y1='130' x2='124' y2='130' stroke='#60a5fa' stroke-width='10'/><line x1='38' y1='70' x2='152' y2='70' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='4 2'/><line x1='38' y1='100' x2='152' y2='100' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='1' stroke-dasharray='4 2'/><text x='156' y='88' font-size='11'>h = 50 mm</text><text x='42' y='15' font-size='10'>p1</text><text x='122' y='15' font-size='10'>p2</text></svg>
figure.caption: U-tube water manometer

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two airways are connected in series (see figure) with resistances $R_1=3$ and $R_2=5\ \mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$, carrying $Q=2\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$. The total pressure drop, in Pa, is _____.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Four airways, each of resistance $1.5\ \mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$, are joined in series as shown. The equivalent resistance, in $\mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$, is _____.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: At junction J (see figure) an air quantity of $10\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$ enters and splits into two branches; one branch carries $6\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$. By the quantity (junction) law, the other branch carries _____ $\mathrm{m^3/s}$.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Kirchhoff's first law: flow in $=$ flow out at a node.

**Working.**
$$Q_2=10-6=4\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$$

**Answer.** $4\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The fan characteristic is $P=150-10Q$ and the mine resistance line is $P=4Q^2$ (see figure). The operating airflow $Q$, in $\mathrm{m^3/s}$, is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The operating point is where fan pressure equals system resistance.

**Working.**
$$150-10Q=4Q^2\Rightarrow 4Q^2+10Q-150=0\Rightarrow 2Q^2+5Q-75=0$$
$$Q=\frac{-5+\sqrt{25+600}}{4}=\frac{-5+25}{4}=5\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$$

**Answer.** $5\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: For the fan curve $P=300-10Q$ working against a mine resistance $P=2Q^2$ (see figure), the operating pressure, in Pa, is _____.
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Solve fan curve against resistance for $Q$, then evaluate $P$.

**Working.**
$$300-10Q=2Q^2\Rightarrow Q^2+5Q-150=0\Rightarrow Q=\frac{-5+\sqrt{25+600}}{2}=10$$
$$P=2\times10^2=200\ \text{Pa}$$

**Answer.** $200$ Pa.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A Pitot tube measures an air velocity of $10\ \mathrm{m/s}$ in an airway (see figure) where the air density is $1.2\ \mathrm{kg/m^3}$. The velocity pressure, in Pa, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='40' width='200' height='60' fill='#e0f2fe' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='70' x2='120' y2='70' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#pf)'/><defs><marker id='pf' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#2563eb'/></marker></defs><path d='M150 70 L150 110 L170 110' fill='none' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2'/><circle cx='150' cy='70' r='3' fill='#dc2626'/><text x='40' y='62' font-size='10' fill='#2563eb'>v = 10 m/s</text><text x='118' y='128' font-size='10' fill='#dc2626'>Pitot tube</text></svg>

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: An airway has the rectangular cross-section shown, 4 m wide by 3 m high. Its hydraulic mean radius ($m=A/O$), in m, is _____ (two decimals).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='40' width='120' height='80' fill='#e0f2fe' stroke='#1f2937' stroke-width='2'/><text x='100' y='138' font-size='11' text-anchor='middle'>width 4 m</text><text x='22' y='84' font-size='11'>3 m</text></svg>

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Air flows at a mean velocity of $3\ \mathrm{m/s}$ through the airway of cross-sectional area $10\ \mathrm{m^2}$ shown. The air quantity, in $\mathrm{m^3/s}$, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='40' width='200' height='60' fill='#e0f2fe' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='70' x2='200' y2='70' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#df)'/><defs><marker id='df' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#2563eb'/></marker></defs><text x='110' y='62' font-size='10' fill='#2563eb'>v = 3 m/s</text><text x='88' y='118' font-size='10'>A = 10 m^2</text></svg>
figure.caption: Airway carrying an air stream

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: An airway (see figure) has resistance $R=0.5\ \mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$ and carries $Q=40\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$. The pressure (mine head) required, in Pa, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='40' width='200' height='60' fill='#e0f2fe' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='70' x2='200' y2='70' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#hf)'/><defs><marker id='hf' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#2563eb'/></marker></defs><text x='70' y='62' font-size='10'>R = 0.5</text><text x='86' y='118' font-size='10' fill='#2563eb'>Q = 40 m^3/s</text></svg>
figure.caption: Airway resistance and flow

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan (see figure) delivers $Q=40\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$ at a pressure of $800\ \mathrm{Pa}$. The air power, in kW, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='70' cy='70' r='40' fill='#e0f2fe' stroke='#475569'/><g stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='3'><line x1='70' y1='70' x2='70' y2='38'/><line x1='70' y1='70' x2='98' y2='86'/><line x1='70' y1='70' x2='42' y2='86'/></g><line x1='112' y1='70' x2='200' y2='70' stroke='#16a34a' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#ff)'/><defs><marker id='ff' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#16a34a'/></marker></defs><text x='120' y='60' font-size='9'>Q = 40 m^3/s</text><text x='120' y='100' font-size='9'>p = 800 Pa</text></svg>
figure.caption: Main fan duty

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two mine resistance lines, $R_1=2$ and $R_2=6\ \mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$, are drawn against the same fan characteristic (see figure). Compared with $R_2$, the airway with $R_1$ will have

figure.caption: One fan curve with two resistances

options: a higher airflow, because lower resistance shifts the operating point to larger Q
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The operating point moves along the fan curve as resistance changes.

**Reasoning.** A lower resistance line is flatter, so it crosses the fan curve at a larger $Q$ (and lower $P$). Hence $R_1$ gives the higher airflow.

**Answer.** Higher airflow.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: In the shaft pair shown, the downcast air column has mean density $1.2\ \mathrm{kg/m^3}$ and the upcast column $1.1\ \mathrm{kg/m^3}$, over a height of 100 m. The natural ventilation pressure, in Pa, is _____ (one decimal).

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 190' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='20' width='30' height='140' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#1d4ed8'/><rect x='170' y='20' width='30' height='140' fill='#fca5a5' stroke='#b91c1c'/><line x1='70' y1='150' x2='170' y2='150' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='8'/><text x='55' y='15' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>downcast</text><text x='185' y='15' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>upcast</text><text x='24' y='95' font-size='9'>1.2</text><text x='205' y='95' font-size='9'>1.1</text><line x1='218' y1='20' x2='218' y2='160' stroke='#dc2626'/><text x='200' y='178' font-size='9' fill='#dc2626'>H = 100 m</text></svg>

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A regulator (see figure) must drop 50 Pa while passing $Q=5\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$. The resistance of the regulator, in $\mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='40' width='200' height='60' fill='#e0f2fe' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='120' y='40' width='8' height='40' fill='#b45309'/><text x='96' y='30' font-size='9'>regulator</text><line x1='30' y1='90' x2='200' y2='90' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#rf)'/><defs><marker id='rf' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#2563eb'/></marker></defs><text x='34' y='112' font-size='9' fill='#2563eb'>Q = 5 m^3/s, drop 50 Pa</text></svg>
figure.caption: Airway with a regulator

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two airways of equal resistance $R_1=R_2=12\ \mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$ are connected in parallel (see figure). The equivalent resistance, in $\mathrm{N\,s^2/m^8}$, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='25' y1='80' x2='70' y2='80' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='3'/><path d='M70 80 C100 30 150 30 180 50' fill='none' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='3'/><path d='M70 80 C100 130 150 130 180 110' fill='none' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='3'/><path d='M180 50 C196 62 196 98 180 110' fill='none' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='3'/><line x1='188' y1='80' x2='215' y2='80' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='3'/><text x='112' y='42' font-size='10'>R1 = 12</text><text x='112' y='132' font-size='10'>R2 = 12</text></svg>
figure.caption: Two equal airways in parallel

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two fans are installed in series in the same airway (see figure), developing 1500 Pa and 1000 Pa respectively at the common airflow. The combined pressure, in Pa, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 260 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='70' cy='60' r='28' fill='#e0f2fe' stroke='#475569'/><circle cx='180' cy='60' r='28' fill='#e0f2fe' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='98' y1='60' x2='152' y2='60' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='3' marker-end='url(#sf)'/><line x1='208' y1='60' x2='250' y2='60' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='3' marker-end='url(#sf)'/><defs><marker id='sf' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#2563eb'/></marker></defs><text x='70' y='105' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>1500 Pa</text><text x='180' y='105' font-size='9' text-anchor='middle'>1000 Pa</text></svg>
figure.caption: Two fans in series

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Fans in series add their pressures at the same flow.

**Working.**
$$P=1500+1000=2500\ \text{Pa}$$

**Answer.** $2500$ Pa.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Air flows round the right-angle bend shown, where the shock-loss factor is $X=1.5$ and the velocity pressure is $p_v=40\ \mathrm{Pa}$. The shock (bend) pressure loss, in Pa, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 180' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><path d='M30 40 L120 40 L120 150' fill='none' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='16'/><path d='M30 40 L120 40 L120 150' fill='none' stroke='#e0f2fe' stroke-width='9'/><line x1='40' y1='40' x2='100' y2='40' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#bf)'/><line x1='120' y1='75' x2='120' y2='135' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#bf)'/><defs><marker id='bf' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#2563eb'/></marker></defs><text x='40' y='95' font-size='9'>X = 1.5</text><text x='40' y='110' font-size='9'>pv = 40 Pa</text></svg>
figure.caption: Right-angle bend in an airway

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A whirling hygrometer (see figure) reads a dry-bulb temperature of $30\,^\circ\mathrm{C}$ and a wet-bulb temperature of $24\,^\circ\mathrm{C}$. The wet-bulb depression, in $^\circ\mathrm{C}$, is _____.

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 180 170' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='50' y='30' width='10' height='90' rx='5' fill='#fca5a5' stroke='#b91c1c'/><circle cx='55' cy='128' r='12' fill='#dc2626'/><text x='28' y='25' font-size='9'>dry 30°C</text><rect x='115' y='50' width='10' height='70' rx='5' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#1d4ed8'/><circle cx='120' cy='128' r='12' fill='#2563eb'/><text x='96' y='45' font-size='9'>wet 24°C</text></svg>
figure.caption: Dry- and wet-bulb thermometers

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Wet-bulb depression $=$ dry-bulb $-$ wet-bulb temperature.

**Working.**
$$\Delta t=30-24=6\,^\circ\mathrm{C}$$

**Answer.** $6\,^\circ\mathrm{C}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The diverging duct fitted at a fan outlet (see figure) is provided mainly to

figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><path d='M20 50 L120 50 L200 20 L200 120 L120 90 L20 90 Z' fill='#e0f2fe' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='30' y1='70' x2='170' y2='70' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#ef)'/><defs><marker id='ef' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#2563eb'/></marker></defs><text x='110' y='132' font-size='10' text-anchor='middle'>diffuser (evasee)</text></svg>
figure.caption: Diverging duct at fan discharge

options: increase the air velocity at exit
recover velocity pressure as static pressure, reducing the velocity (kinetic) energy lost at discharge
questions.solution:
**Concept.** An evasee is a gradually expanding outlet.

**Reasoning.** As area increases, velocity falls and part of the velocity pressure is converted back to static pressure, lowering the kinetic energy (velocity head) discarded to atmosphere and improving fan efficiency.

**Answer.** Recover velocity pressure as static pressure.

questions.stem: A fan passes $30\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$ at $600\ \text{rpm}$. Using the fan laws, compute the quantity at $900\ \text{rpm}$ ($\mathrm{m^3/s}$).

figure.caption: Fan law: quantity vs speed
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='80' cy='65' r='34' fill='#dbeafe' stroke='#475569'/><g stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'><line x1='80' y1='65' x2='80' y2='35'/><line x1='80' y1='65' x2='106' y2='80'/><line x1='80' y1='65' x2='54' y2='80'/></g><path d='M120 40 A30 30 0 0 1 120 90' fill='none' stroke='#16a34a' marker-end='url(#sp)'/><defs><marker id='sp' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#16a34a'/></marker></defs><text x='30' y='118' font-size='8'>600 -> 900 rpm</text></svg>

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan develops $1000\ \text{Pa}$ at $600\ \text{rpm}$. Using the fan laws, compute the pressure at $900\ \text{rpm}$ (Pa).

figure.caption: Fan law: pressure vs speed squared
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='80' cy='65' r='34' fill='#dbeafe' stroke='#475569'/><g stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'><line x1='80' y1='65' x2='80' y2='35'/><line x1='80' y1='65' x2='106' y2='80'/><line x1='80' y1='65' x2='54' y2='80'/></g><path d='M120 40 A30 30 0 0 1 120 90' fill='none' stroke='#16a34a' marker-end='url(#sp2)'/><defs><marker id='sp2' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#16a34a'/></marker></defs><text x='30' y='118' font-size='8'>P varies with N^2</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Pressure varies with the square of speed: $P_2=P_1\left(\dfrac{N_2}{N_1}\right)^2$.

**Working.**
$$P_2=1000\left(\frac{900}{600}\right)^2=1000\times2.25=2250\ \text{Pa}$$

**Answer.** $2250$ Pa.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A fan absorbs $20\ \text{kW}$ at $600\ \text{rpm}$. Using the fan laws, compute the power at $900\ \text{rpm}$ (kW).

figure.caption: Fan law: power vs speed cubed
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='80' cy='65' r='34' fill='#dbeafe' stroke='#475569'/><g stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'><line x1='80' y1='65' x2='80' y2='35'/><line x1='80' y1='65' x2='106' y2='80'/><line x1='80' y1='65' x2='54' y2='80'/></g><path d='M120 40 A30 30 0 0 1 120 90' fill='none' stroke='#16a34a' marker-end='url(#sp3)'/><defs><marker id='sp3' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#16a34a'/></marker></defs><text x='30' y='118' font-size='8'>W varies with N^3</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Power varies with the cube of speed: $W_2=W_1\left(\dfrac{N_2}{N_1}\right)^3$.

**Working.**
$$W_2=20\left(\frac{900}{600}\right)^3=20\times3.375=67.5\ \text{kW}$$

**Answer.** $67.5$ kW.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: In the two-shaft system shown, natural ventilation is driven mainly by:

figure.caption: Downcast and upcast columns
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='40' y='20' width='20' height='120' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='160' y='20' width='20' height='120' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='60' y1='130' x2='160' y2='130' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='50' y1='110' x2='50' y2='40' stroke='#dc2626' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#nv)'/><line x1='170' y1='40' x2='170' y2='110' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#nv)'/><defs><marker id='nv' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#475569'/></marker></defs><text x='28' y='154' font-size='8'>downcast vs upcast shaft</text></svg>

options: the density difference between the cooler downcast column and the warmer upcast column
the rotation of the earth acting on the air
the magnetic field of the orebody
capillary action in the rock pores
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Natural ventilation pressure arises from unequal air-column weights.

**Reasoning.** The downcast air is cooler and denser while the upcast air is warmer and lighter; the difference in column weights creates a motive pressure that circulates air without a fan.

**Answer.** The density difference between the downcast and upcast columns.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The fan characteristic is $P=400-20Q$ (Pa, with $Q$ in $\mathrm{m^3/s}$) and the mine resistance gives $P=12\,Q^2$. Read/compute the operating-point air quantity ($\mathrm{m^3/s}$).
questions.solution:
**Concept.** The operating point is where fan pressure equals mine resistance pressure.

**Working.**
$$400-20Q=12Q^2\Rightarrow 12Q^2+20Q-400=0\Rightarrow Q=5\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$$
Check: $400-20(5)=300$ and $12(5)^2=300$.

**Answer.** $5\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two booster fans developing $1500\ \text{Pa}$ and $1000\ \text{Pa}$ at the operating quantity are placed in series. Compute the combined total pressure (Pa).

figure.caption: Fans in series
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 110' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><g fill='#dbeafe' stroke='#475569'><circle cx='70' cy='55' r='24'/><circle cx='150' cy='55' r='24'/></g><line x1='10' y1='55' x2='46' y2='55' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='94' y1='55' x2='126' y2='55' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='174' y1='55' x2='220' y2='55' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#sf)'/><defs><marker id='sf' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#2563eb'/></marker></defs><text x='40' y='95' font-size='8'>series: 1500 + 1000 Pa</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Fans in series add their pressures at the common quantity.

**Working.**
$$P=1500+1000=2500\ \text{Pa}$$

**Answer.** $2500$ Pa.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Two identical fans, each passing $25\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$ at the common pressure, are connected in parallel. Compute the combined quantity ($\mathrm{m^3/s}$).

figure.caption: Fans in parallel
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><circle cx='110' cy='40' r='22' fill='#dbeafe' stroke='#475569'/><circle cx='110' cy='100' r='22' fill='#dbeafe' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='10' y1='70' x2='60' y2='70' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M60 70 L60 40 L88 40' fill='none' stroke='#475569'/><path d='M60 70 L60 100 L88 100' fill='none' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='132' y1='40' x2='190' y2='70' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='132' y1='100' x2='190' y2='70' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='190' y1='70' x2='230' y2='70' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#pf)'/><defs><marker id='pf' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#2563eb'/></marker></defs><text x='40' y='130' font-size='8'>parallel: 25 + 25 m^3/s</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** Fans in parallel add their quantities at the common pressure.

**Working.**
$$Q=25+25=50\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$$

**Answer.** $50\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: Kirchhoff's second (pressure) law, applied to the closed mesh shown in a ventilation network, states that:

figure.caption: Closed mesh in a network
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 150' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><polygon points='40,75 110,30 180,75 110,120' fill='none' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><g fill='#1f2937'><circle cx='40' cy='75' r='3'/><circle cx='110' cy='30' r='3'/><circle cx='180' cy='75' r='3'/><circle cx='110' cy='120' r='3'/></g><text x='62' y='48' font-size='8'>mesh</text><text x='38' y='142' font-size='8'>algebraic sum of drops = 0</text></svg>

options: the algebraic sum of pressure drops around any closed mesh is zero
the sum of quantities entering a junction is always zero
the resistance of any mesh equals the fan pressure
all branches in a mesh carry equal quantity
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Kirchhoff's pressure (mesh) law is an energy-conservation statement.

**Reasoning.** Going once around any closed loop returns to the same pressure, so the algebraic sum of the pressure drops (including any fan pressure rises taken as negative) must be zero.

**Answer.** The algebraic sum of pressure drops around any closed mesh is zero.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: A total airflow of $30\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$ splits naturally between two parallel airways of resistance $R_1=0.1$ and $R_2=0.4\ \mathrm{Ns^2/m^8}$. Compute the quantity through the higher-resistance airway ($\mathrm{m^3/s}$).

figure.caption: Natural splitting of airflow
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 140' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><line x1='10' y1='70' x2='60' y2='70' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='3' marker-end='url(#sa)'/><path d='M60 70 L60 35 L190 35' fill='none' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M60 70 L60 105 L190 105' fill='none' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><line x1='190' y1='35' x2='230' y2='35' stroke='#16a34a' stroke-width='3' marker-end='url(#sa)'/><line x1='190' y1='105' x2='230' y2='105' stroke='#16a34a' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#sa)'/><defs><marker id='sa' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#475569'/></marker></defs><text x='100' y='30' font-size='8'>R1=0.1</text><text x='100' y='125' font-size='8'>R2=0.4, total Q=30</text></svg>

questions.solution:
**Concept.** In a free split, equal pressure drop gives $\dfrac{Q_1}{Q_2}=\sqrt{\dfrac{R_2}{R_1}}$.

**Working.**
$$\frac{Q_1}{Q_2}=\sqrt{\frac{0.4}{0.1}}=2\Rightarrow Q_1=2Q_2,\ Q_1+Q_2=30\Rightarrow Q_2=10\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$$

**Answer.** $10\ \mathrm{m^3/s}$.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: In the forcing auxiliary system shown (fan and rigid duct to a heading), the air quantity actually reaching the face is generally:

figure.caption: Forcing auxiliary duct
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 240 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='20' y='40' width='200' height='40' fill='#e5e7eb' stroke='#475569'/><rect x='30' y='50' width='150' height='14' fill='#dbeafe' stroke='#2563eb'/><circle cx='40' cy='57' r='8' fill='#dbeafe' stroke='#475569'/><text x='185' y='62' font-size='8'>face</text><text x='30' y='100' font-size='8'>forcing duct to heading</text></svg>

options: less than the fan output, because of duct leakage
greater than the fan output, because of duct suction
exactly equal to the fan output in all cases
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Ducted auxiliary systems suffer air leakage along their length.

**Reasoning.** Joints and small openings leak air, so the quantity delivered at the face is less than the quantity the fan puts into the duct; the ratio is the duct's effective delivery (leakage) factor.

**Answer.** Less than the fan output, because of duct leakage.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: As intake air descends a deep shaft (figure), autocompression causes the air temperature to:

figure.caption: Autocompression with depth
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 160' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='90' y='20' width='16' height='120' fill='#cbd5e1' stroke='#475569'/><line x1='98' y1='40' x2='98' y2='120' stroke='#2563eb' stroke-width='2' marker-end='url(#ac)'/><defs><marker id='ac' markerWidth='8' markerHeight='8' refX='6' refY='3' orient='auto'><path d='M0 0 L6 3 L0 6 Z' fill='#2563eb'/></marker></defs><text x='30' y='30' font-size='8'>surface</text><text x='28' y='135' font-size='8'>depth increases</text></svg>

options: rise, as the air is compressed by the increasing column weight
fall, because deeper air is always cooler
rise only if a fan is running
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Autocompression converts potential energy of descending air into enthalpy.

**Reasoning.** As air moves down the shaft the rising pressure compresses it, raising its temperature even before any strata or machinery heat is added.

**Answer.** Rise, as the air is compressed by the increasing column weight.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The single opening shown is used to characterise how 'tight' or 'free' a mine is to ventilate (figure). The equivalent orifice is:

figure.caption: Equivalent orifice
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 200 120' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='30' width='140' height='60' fill='#94a3b8' stroke='#475569'/><circle cx='100' cy='60' r='14' fill='#ffffff' stroke='#475569'/><text x='34' y='108' font-size='8'>equivalent orifice of a mine</text></svg>

options: the area of a sharp-edged orifice that would pass the same quantity under the same pressure as the whole mine
the total cross-sectional area of all airways added together
the area of the main fan drift
the perimeter of the largest airway
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Equivalent orifice expresses mine resistance as an aperture.

**Reasoning.** It is the area of an imaginary sharp-edged orifice that would pass the same air quantity at the same pressure difference as the entire mine; a large orifice means a free mine, a small one a tight mine.

**Answer.** The area of a sharp-edged orifice that would pass the same quantity under the same pressure as the whole mine.

questions.subject: Mine Ventilation
questions.stem: The fan installed within a main airway shown (figure) is used to raise airflow in one branch of the circuit. The chief design caution is that it must:

figure.caption: Booster fan in an airway
figure.markup: <svg viewBox='0 0 220 130' xmlns='http://www.w3.org/2000/svg'><rect x='30' y='40' width='160' height='40' fill='#bfdbfe' stroke='#475569'/><circle cx='110' cy='60' r='16' fill='none' stroke='#475569' stroke-width='2'/><path d='M110 60 L110 46 L120 56 Z' fill='#475569'/><text x='34' y='110' font-size='8'>booster fan in an airway</text></svg>

options: be sized and positioned to boost the branch flow without causing uncontrolled recirculation
always reverse the airflow direction in the district
replace the main surface fan entirely
operate only when the main fan is stopped
questions.solution:
**Concept.** Booster fans assist underground circuits.

**Reasoning.** A booster fan increases quantity in part of the network, but if oversized or poorly placed it can draw return air back into the intake, causing uncontrolled recirculation; correct sizing and location prevent this.

**Answer.** Be sized and positioned to boost the branch flow without causing uncontrolled recirculation.
